title: Use standard remove functions for removing pod ctrs
body: Instead of rewriting the logic, reuse the standard logic we use for removing containers, which is much better tested.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This *theoretically* will fix the bad CGroup flakes on master, but I haven't tested it for that yet.
Pod tests still failing, amongst everything else
Added a commit to fix the other CI issues (thanks @baude for finding them)
The pod tests are back to failing...

Current status: I think I made it worse. Cleanup processes are still firing and now they have even more time to start up.

Per discussion with @jwhonce and @baude we have a way forward. Set the PID limit to 0 on the CGroups in question, so they stop accepting forks, so no cleanup processes.

If this fails, we freeze the cgroup, SIGKILL conmon, unfreeze it, and conmon has no opportunity to cleanup.

This is very much a Monday thing. Very, very much.
Added a commit to set PIDs limit to 0, which seems to resolve the races locally on my laptop. Let's see if CI agrees.

LGTM is gone, so I'll unhold

/hold cancel
It's still broken... Exact same errors. Is this even a race against the cleanup process? We inhibited forking...
Nevermind. Kernel interprets a PID limit of 0 as unlimited. Setting to 1 fixes locally. Repushing.

The tests are passing.
@baude @rhatdan @jwhonce @vrothberg Someone want to merge this to fix CI on master?
